title: Inert Prime Area Quantization PASS: p=3, k=2 (mod 9) — anchor case (reduces to [108])
_fixture_purpose: Anchor fixture. p=3, k=2, modulus=9. This is the same arithmetic fact certified in family [108] (QA_AREA_QUANTIZATION_CERT.v1), now re-stated as an instance of the general inert-prime theorem QA_AREA_QUANTIZATION_PK_CERT.v1. Forbidden set = {3,6} = {r : v_3(r)=1} — the residues divisible by 3 but not 9. This anchor certifies that the general theorem reduces to the known mod-9 result, establishing backward consistency with [108].

inert_witness.irreducibility_check: x^2+x-1 mod 3: x=0 -> -1≡2, x=1 -> 1, x=2 -> 4+2-1=5≡2. No roots mod 3. Irreducible.
inert_witness.notes: Legendre(5,3): 5≡2 mod 3; 2^((3-1)/2)=2^1=2≡-1 mod 3. QNR confirmed. Equivalently: 3 is inert in Z[phi] because Z[phi]/3Z[phi] ≅ GF(9).

theorem_claim.statement: For p=3 (inert in Z[phi]) and k=2: Im(f) = {r in Z/9Z : v_3(r) != 1} = {0,1,2,4,5,7,8}. Forbidden = {r : 3|r but 9∤r} = {3,6}.
theorem_claim.forbidden_description: Residues with v_3(r)=1: divisible by 3 but not 9. These cannot be norms of elements in Z[phi]/9Z[phi] because 3 is inert (Z[phi]/3Z[phi] ≅ GF(9)); any element with norm divisible by 3 must have norm divisible by 9.

validation_checks.description: p=3 is inert in Z[phi]: Legendre(5,3)=-1
